options: Rammohan Roy and Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar
questions.explanation: Rammohan Roy and Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar led the socio-reform movement. They worked to reform Indian society and abolish social evils.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was founded in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885. It was founded by A.O. Hume and others.

questions.question: Who among the following was the first Governor-General of independent India?
questions.explanation: Lord Mountbatten served as the first Governor-General of independent India from August 15, 1947, to June 1948.

questions.question: The States Reorganisation Act of 1956 was primarily based on the recommendations of which commission?
questions.explanation: The States Reorganisation Commission, headed by Fazl Ali, recommended the reorganization of states on a linguistic basis in 1956.

questions.question: In which year did India conduct its first peaceful nuclear explosion at Pokhran?
questions.explanation: India's first peaceful nuclear test, codenamed 'Smiling Buddha', was conducted at Pokhran, Rajasthan in 1974.

questions.question: The 'Tamil Eelam' demand for a separate state was associated with which militant group in Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: The Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam (LTTE) fought for an independent Tamil state in northern and eastern Sri Lanka.

questions.question: Which Indian Prime Minister signed the historic 'Treaty of Peace, Friendship and Cooperation' with the USSR in 1971?
questions.explanation: The Indo-Soviet Treaty of 1971, a landmark in India's foreign policy, was signed by Prime Minister Indira Gandhi.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress party split in 1969 primarily over which issue?
questions.explanation: The 1969 split was triggered by the party's syndicate opposing Prime Minister Indira Gandhi's decision to nationalize banks.

questions.question: Operation Vijay (1999) was launched by India to evict infiltrators from which region?
questions.explanation: India launched Operation Vijay in 1999 to recapture positions in the Kargil district occupied by Pakistani intruders.

questions.question: The White Revolution in India, aimed at increasing milk production, is most closely associated with which organization?
questions.explanation: The National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) led Operation Flood, the program behind India's White Revolution.

questions.question: Article 370 of the Indian Constitution, which granted special autonomous status, was originally applicable to which state?
questions.explanation: Article 370 granted special autonomous status to the former state of Jammu and Kashmir; it was abrogated in 2019.

questions.question: The 'Poona Pact' of 1932, an important pre-independence event, was an agreement between whom?
questions.explanation: The Poona Pact was an agreement between Mahatma Gandhi and B.R. Ambedkar on the representation of depressed classes in legislatures.

questions.question: Who was the first Governor-General of independent India?
questions.explanation: Lord Mountbatten served as the first Governor-General of independent India from August 15, 1947, to June 1948.

questions.question: In which year was the Indian Constitution adopted by the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution was adopted on November 26, 1949, and came into effect on January 26, 1950.

questions.question: Which princely state's accession to India required a police action named 'Operation Polo'?
questions.explanation: Operation Polo was the Indian military operation in September 1948 to annex the princely state of Hyderabad.

questions.question: Who was the first Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu (then Madras State) after independence?
questions.explanation: O. P. Ramaswamy Reddiyar served as the first Chief Minister of Madras State from 1947 to 1949.

questions.question: The 'Dravidian movement' in Tamil Nadu was initially led by which social reformer?
questions.explanation: Periyar E. V. Ramasamy founded the Self-Respect Movement and was the leading figure of the early Dravidian movement.

questions.question: Which Indian Prime Minister signed the 'Shimla Agreement' with Pakistan in 1972?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi signed the Shimla Agreement with Zulfikar Ali Bhutto in 1972 to normalize relations after the Bangladesh Liberation War.

questions.question: The 'Green Revolution' in India during the 1960s was most closely associated with which crop?
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution focused on high-yielding varieties of wheat, particularly in Punjab, Haryana, and Uttar Pradesh.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu leader served as the Union Minister of Defence during the 1965 Indo-Pak war?
questions.explanation: V. K. Krishna Menon, a Tamil Nadu-born politician, was the Defence Minister of India during the 1965 war.

questions.question: The 'Anti-Hindi agitations' of Tamil Nadu in 1965 were primarily a protest against which policy?
options: Imposition of Hindi as official language
questions.explanation: The agitations opposed the central government's plan to make Hindi the sole official language of India from 1965.

questions.question: The first Governor-General of independent India was?
questions.explanation: Lord Mountbatten served as the first Governor-General of independent India from August 15, 1947, to June 1948.

questions.question: The princely state of Hyderabad was integrated into India in 1948 through?
questions.explanation: Operation Polo, launched in September 1948, led to the annexation of Hyderabad by the Indian Army.

questions.question: The first linguistic state created in India after independence was?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh was formed in 1953 from the Telugu-speaking areas of Madras State, following the fast of Potti Sriramulu.

questions.question: The Indian Constitution was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly adopted the Constitution on 26 November 1949, and it came into effect on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: The first Five-Year Plan of India (1951-1956) focused primarily on?
questions.explanation: The first Five-Year Plan prioritized agriculture, including irrigation and power projects, to address food scarcity.

questions.question: The 'Goa Liberation' from Portuguese rule occurred in which year?
questions.explanation: India liberated Goa through Operation Vijay in December 1961, ending 451 years of Portuguese colonial rule.

questions.question: The 'Tashkent Agreement' of 1966 was signed between India and which country?
questions.explanation: The Tashkent Agreement was signed between India and Pakistan in January 1966 to resolve the 1965 war, with Soviet mediation.

questions.question: The 'Green Revolution' in India during the 1960s was most closely associated with the introduction of?
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution relied on high-yielding variety (HYV) seeds, especially for wheat and rice, to boost agricultural output.

questions.question: Which Indian state was created in 1960 on a linguistic basis for Marathi-speaking people?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra was formed on May 1, 1960, following the bifurcation of the bilingual Bombay State.

questions.question: The 'Green Revolution' in India was most closely associated with the increased production of which crop?
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution, led by M.S. Swaminathan, focused on high-yielding varieties of wheat, especially in Punjab.

questions.question: The 'Anti-Hindi Agitations' of 1965 in Tamil Nadu were primarily against which policy?
options: Imposition of Hindi as sole official language
questions.explanation: The agitations opposed the central government's plan to make Hindi the sole official language from January 26, 1965.

questions.question: Which Indian state was the first to be formed on a linguistic basis in 1953?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh was created in 1953 from the Telugu-speaking areas of Madras State after Potti Sriramulu's fast.

questions.question: The 'Pokhran-II' nuclear tests were conducted in which year?
questions.explanation: India conducted a series of five nuclear tests at Pokhran in May 1998, codenamed 'Operation Shakti'.

questions.question: The States Reorganisation Act was passed in which year?
questions.explanation: The States Reorganisation Act of 1956 reorganized states on linguistic lines, effective November 1, 1956.

questions.question: The first Five-Year Plan in India began in which year?
questions.explanation: India's first Five-Year Plan was launched in 1951, focusing on agriculture.

questions.question: The 'Green Revolution' in India is primarily associated with which crop?
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution initially focused on high-yielding varieties of wheat, leading to a significant increase in production.

questions.question: India's policy of Non-Alignment was first articulated by which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru was the chief architect of India's non-alignment policy during the Cold War.

questions.question: The state of Tamil Nadu was officially renamed from Madras State in which year?
questions.explanation: Madras State was renamed Tamil Nadu in 1969 under the DMK government led by C.N. Annadurai.

questions.question: Who was the first Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu under the DMK government?
questions.explanation: C.N. Annadurai became the first DMK Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu in 1967.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress split occurred in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress split into Congress (R) and Congress (O) in 1969 over ideological differences.

questions.question: The 'Garibi Hatao' slogan was used by which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi used the 'Garibi Hatao' (Remove Poverty) slogan during the 1971 general election campaign.

questions.question: In which year was the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) founded?
questions.explanation: The DMK was founded by C.N. Annadurai in 1949 after splitting from the Dravidar Kazhagam.

questions.question: Which Indian state was formed in 1960 by dividing the bilingual state of Bombay?
questions.explanation: The Bombay Reorganisation Act, 1960, created the separate states of Gujarat and Maharashtra from the former Bombay State.

questions.question: The 'Goa Liberation' from Portuguese rule took place in which year?
questions.explanation: Indian armed forces liberated Goa from Portuguese colonial rule on December 19, 1961, through Operation Vijay.

questions.question: The 'Green Revolution' in India was most successful in increasing the production of which crop?
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution, led by M.S. Swaminathan, dramatically boosted wheat production, especially in Punjab, Haryana, and Uttar Pradesh.

questions.question: Which year did India conduct its first successful nuclear test, codenamed 'Smiling Buddha'?
questions.explanation: India's first nuclear test, 'Smiling Buddha', was conducted at Pokhran, Rajasthan, on May 18, 1974.

questions.question: The 'Anti-Hindi agitations' of 1965 in Tamil Nadu were primarily a protest against which policy?
options: Imposition of Hindi as the sole official language
questions.explanation: The agitations opposed the central government's plan to make Hindi the sole official language of India from 1965, leading to the Official Languages Act.

questions.question: Who was the first Indian to serve as the Governor-General of independent India?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari became the first and only Indian Governor-General of India in 1948 after Lord Mountbatten.

questions.question: In which year was the States Reorganisation Commission established to recommend the redrawing of state boundaries on linguistic basis?
questions.explanation: The States Reorganisation Commission was formed in 1953 under Justice Fazl Ali, leading to the States Reorganisation Act of 1956.

questions.question: Which state was the first to be created on a linguistic basis after independence?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh was created in 1953 from the Telugu-speaking areas of Madras State following the Potti Sriramulu agitation.

questions.question: Who was the first Chief Minister of Madras State after the adoption of the Constitution of India in 1950?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari became the first Chief Minister of Madras State in 1952 after the first general elections under the new Constitution.

questions.question: Which leader is credited with the successful integration of over 560 princely states into the Indian Union?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, as Home Minister, led the integration of princely states with the help of V. P. Menon and Mountbatten.

questions.question: The anti-Hindi agitation in Tamil Nadu reached its peak in which year, leading to widespread protests and violence?
questions.explanation: In 1965, massive protests erupted against the imposition of Hindi as the sole official language, leading to a policy of continued English usage.

questions.question: Which Indian leader is most closely associated with the Green Revolution that began in the 1960s?
questions.explanation: M. S. Swaminathan, an Indian agronomist, was the architect of the Green Revolution, introducing high-yielding wheat varieties in collaboration with Norman Borlaug.

questions.question: The First Five-Year Plan (1951-1956) in India primarily focused on which sector?
questions.explanation: The First Five-Year Plan aimed at agricultural development and irrigation to address food shortages and boost rural economy.

questions.question: Madras State was officially renamed Tamil Nadu in which year?
questions.explanation: The name was changed to Tamil Nadu on January 14, 1969, under the Chief Ministership of C. N. Annadurai.

questions.question: Which political party, formed in 1949, played a key role in the Dravidian movement and later formed the first non-Congress government in Tamil Nadu?
options: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (AIADMK)
questions.explanation: The DMK was founded in 1949 by C. N. Annadurai after splitting from the Dravidar Kazhagam, and came to power in Tamil Nadu in 1967.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a Finance Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 280 of the Constitution provides for the formation of a Finance Commission every five years to recommend distribution of tax revenues between the Union and the States.

questions.question: The state of Andhra Pradesh was created on linguistic basis in which year?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh was formed on October 1, 1953, after the death of Potti Sriramulu, becoming the first state created on a linguistic basis.

questions.question: The first linguistic state to be formed in independent India was:
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh was formed in 1953 on linguistic lines for Telugu speakers, following the death of Potti Sriramulu.
